reason: First generation (1940s-50s) used vacuum tubes; transistors define the second generation.
By definition an impact printer's print head physically strikes the paper.
Hardware is the term for the physical components of a computer.
Disk drive is the only storage device among the options; keyboard/monitor/printer are I/O.
QWERTY names the standard keyboard layout.
Officially Digital Versatile Disc; 'Digital Video Disc' was the early informal name, so B is the intended key.
Monitor is an output device; keyboard, joystick, microphone are inputs.
The CPU processes data into information and is built from integrated circuits.
An Uninterruptible Power Supply supplies backup power during outages.
Only Plotter/Printer/Monitor are all outputs; other groups contain scanner, keyboard, or mouse (inputs).
Buses are the motherboard pathways carrying data/address/control signals between components.
A card cage is the frame/chassis into which circuit boards are slotted and mounted.
A printer renders computer data to paper, i.e. an output device.
Printer is the only output device; scanner, mouse, keyboard are inputs.
A chip (smart) card embeds memory and a microprocessor in a credit-card form factor.
The physical structure of a computer is its hardware.
Two rapid presses of the left button is a double-click.
Notebooks, cell phones and PDAs are programmable computing devices; a digital scanner is a peripheral input device.
A single bus minimizes wiring/cost and lets peripherals attach easily; it sacrifices speed, so options citing speed are wrong.
Buffer registers hold data so processor and slower I/O devices can each transfer at their own rate.
